topic: An artist's work is displayed without their permission
raw_output:
[story 1]

The gallery lights felt too harsh, illuminating the canvas where my swirling blues and deep violets seemed to mock my very soul. I stood near the entrance, a knot tightening in my chest, watching strangers drift past the display. A young woman, her eyes wide with a sort of stunned awe, stopped before a piece I’d poured months of quiet yearning into. She reached out, her fingers hovering just above the textured surface, a soft, almost reverent expression on her face. A warmth spread through my palms, a gentle easing of the tension that had been coiling in my stomach. I managed a small, almost imperceptible nod, a silent acknowledgment of the connection, before retreating into the shadows of the corner, feeling a quiet sense of acceptance wash over me.

[story 2]

It was a Tuesday, and the stark white walls of the exhibition felt cold against my skin. Then I saw it—my portrait, hanging prominently, bathed in an unforgiving spotlight. A collector, impeccably dressed, circled it slowly, his gaze lingering on the details I had labored over. He didn't speak, just tilted his head, a look of deep contemplation softening the sharp lines around his eyes. I watched him, my breath catching slightly in my throat. He finally stepped closer, his silence speaking volumes. When he finally turned to leave, a subtle shift occurred within me; the heavy burden I’d been carrying seemed to lighten, replaced by a gentle, unexpected buoyancy. I allowed myself a slow, deliberate exhale, a small, private smile touching the corners of my mouth.

[story 3]

The murmur of the crowd was overwhelming, a tidal wave of appraisal and speculation. I was positioned near the refreshments, trying to maintain a neutral expression, but my hands were trembling slightly as I observed the reactions to my work. Then, a small child, perhaps seven years old, pointed at a depiction of a stormy sea. The child’s expression was pure wonder, unburdened by judgment. As the little girl traced the outline of a wave with a tiny finger, a profound sense of peace settled over me. It felt as if a heavy stone had been lifted from my shoulders. I found myself moving towards her, my steps unhurried, offering a soft, encouraging look that I hoped conveyed everything words failed to articulate.
